title: Non-Overlapping Intervals
description: Given a collection of intervals, find the minimum number of intervals you need to remove to make the rest of the intervals non-overlapping.

examples.explanation: By removing the interval [1,3], the remaining intervals [[1,2],[2,3],[3,4]] do not overlap.
We can keep one interval and remove the other two overlapping intervals, thus making them non-overlapping.
The intervals [1,2] and [2,3] are already non-overlapping, so no intervals need to be removed.

hints: Consider sorting the intervals based on their end times.
Keep track of the end of the last added interval to the non-overlapping set.
If an interval overlaps, decide whether to remove it based on its end time.
Think about how many intervals overlap with each other to determine the minimum removals.
